explanation.summary: Fats are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in S tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in S tier with an overall score of 62 from a base nutrition score of 58. Context lifted it by 4 points.

topPros.title: strong all-round legume profile
topPros.explanation: Fibre, minerals, and useful protein all show up well enough to make it a very solid category food.

topPros.title: easy to use across lots of meals
topPros.explanation: Works in bowls, curries, salads, hummus, and snacks.

topPros.title: pretty filling and steady
topPros.explanation: Useful as a practical staple rather than just a niche health food.

topCons.title: sugar is a bit high for the category
topCons.explanation: Not a disaster, but it is less clean than the best legumes on this axis.

topCons.title: can be rough on some people digestively
contains some anti-nutrients
topCons.explanation: Phytates may reduce mineral uptake somewhat.

narrationNotes: Context improved the final tier case by 4 points.

closing.summary: Chickpeas has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

scenes.narrationText: 6g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a legume, this is exactly the kind of profile you want.
scenes.subtitleText: 6g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a legume, this is exactly the kind of profile you want.

scenes.narrationText: 60.7g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.
scenes.subtitleText: 60.7g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.

scenes.narrationText: 19.3g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.
scenes.subtitleText: 19.3g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.
